Claim: HANA-X Copilot is the first commercially available SAP S/4HANA Copilot

Status: Published under founder authorization; evidence package pending

Claim: HANA-X Copilot supports a 100% on-premises deployment model
Required evidence: Deployment architecture and validated bill of materials
Status: Approved wording published; architecture evidence pending
Approved wording: HANA-X Copilot supports a 100% on-premises deployment model.
Notes: Publication wording avoids a universal deployment guarantee while preserving the product thesis. Active exception: ICE-FM-001_v1.0_Publication-Risk-Acceptance.md.

Claim: HANA-X Copilot preserves SAP Clean Core principles by using released SAP integration interfaces
Required evidence: Official SAP Clean Core guidance and endpoint mapping
Status: Approved wording published; SAP architecture review pending
Approved wording: It preserves Clean Core principles by integrating through released and supported SAP S/4HANA APIs, OData services, business events, and other approved integration interfaces appropriate to the customer's S/4HANA edition and release.
Notes: Edition- and release-specific qualification retained. Active exception: ICE-FM-001_v1.0_Publication-Risk-Acceptance.md.

Claim: The core solution can operate without SAP BTP AI services, hyperscaler-hosted LLMs, or consumption-based AI credits
Required evidence: Deployment architecture, licensing analysis, and legal review
Status: Approved wording published; licensing evidence pending
Approved wording: The core HANA-X Copilot solution can be deployed without requiring SAP BTP AI services, hyperscaler-hosted language models, or consumption-based AI credits.
Notes: Applies to the core solution; customer-specific architectures may still use those services by choice. Active exception: ICE-FM-001_v1.0_Publication-Risk-Acceptance.md.

Claim: HANA-X Copilot can plan and execute governed multi-step SAP procurement workflows
Required evidence: Working demonstration, test evidence, and endpoint mapping
Notes: Separate implemented capability from target-state examples

Claim: SAP cloud-backlog momentum is a material investor signal
Required evidence: SAP earnings releases, investor presentation, dated market analysis, and comparable definitions
Approved wording: Cloud-backlog momentum is a closely watched indicator of contracted future cloud revenue.
Notes: Do not imply HANA-X alone can reverse SAP backlog trends.

Claim: Agentic AI positioning is reshaping enterprise software competition
Required evidence: Official competitor product materials, adoption disclosures, and independent research
Approved wording: Enterprise software competition is moving toward agentic systems that interpret intent and coordinate work.
Notes: Avoid declaring competitor failure or market leadership without category definition and current evidence.

Claim: Oracle RPO scale creates competitive narrative pressure
Required evidence: Oracle filing or earnings release, RPO definition, reporting date, and comparable basis
Approved wording: Oracle's large contracted commitments reinforce the market narrative around AI infrastructure scale.
Notes: Exact USD 638 billion figure requires primary-source verification for the applicable reporting period.

Claim: HANA-X Copilot can improve SAP adoption utilization and workflow velocity
Required evidence: Customer baseline, controlled pilot, task telemetry, and user research
Approved wording: HANA-X Copilot is designed to reduce interaction friction and support faster governed completion of SAP work.
Notes: Quantitative improvement claims require measured pilots.

Claim: Customer-controlled local deployment can improve sovereignty and cost predictability
Required evidence: Validated deployment architecture, TCO model, security review, and customer requirements
Approved wording: Customer-controlled local deployment provides an option for greater data-processing control and infrastructure cost predictability.
Notes: Do not describe on-premises AI as free; include hardware and operating costs.

Claim: Clean Core-aligned integration can reduce extension and upgrade risk
Required evidence: Official SAP guidance, endpoint mapping, implementation evidence, and upgrade analysis
Approved wording: Keeping agent logic outside the ERP core and using governed released interfaces can reduce extension and upgrade risk.
Notes: Each tool remains edition and release specific; no universal guarantee.
